requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must have qualified business income from pass-through entities (sole proprietorship, partnership, S Corp) to claim up to 20% deduction of QBI subject to income thresholds, W-2 wages, and property limitations
criteria: Qualified business income from Schedule C, K-1 (partnership, S Corp), or rental real estate (if trade or business)
2025 thresholds: Full 20% deduction if taxable income below $197,300 (single) / $394,600 (MFJ)
Above thresholds, specified service trade or business (SSTB) income phases out; non-SSTB subject to W-2/property limits
SSTB includes health, law, accounting, actuarial, performing arts, consulting, athletics, financial/brokerage services
W-2 wage limitation (above threshold): Greater of 50% of W-2 wages OR 25% wages + 2.5% of UBIA of qualified property
Qualified property includes tangible depreciable property used in business and within depreciation period
Can aggregate multiple trades or businesses to optimize W-2 wage and property limitations
Rental real estate can qualify as trade or business if meets safe harbor (250+ hours, maintain records) or facts and circumstances

qbi_deduction_rules.deduction_amount: Lesser of (1) 20% of QBI, or (2) 20% of taxable income less net capital gain
qbi_deduction_rules.sstb_limitation: Specified service businesses lose QBI deduction as income exceeds thresholds
qbi_deduction_rules.w2_wage_limitation: Above threshold, deduction limited to greater of 50% W-2 wages or 25% wages + 2.5% UBIA
qbi_deduction_rules.aggregation_election: Can aggregate related businesses to optimize wage/property limitations
qbi_deduction_rules.rental_safe_harbor: Rental real estate qualifies with 250+ hours and contemporaneous records

restrictions: W-2 wages of S Corporation reduce QBI but provide deduction limitation relief for W-2 wage test
Reasonable compensation requirement for S Corp affects both QBI amount and W-2 wage limitation
Capital gains, interest, dividends, and investment income not qualified business income
Qualified REIT dividends and PTP income receive separate 20% deduction calculation
SSTB determination based on principal asset being reputation/skill of owners or employees
Aggregation election binding for current and future years; must meet common ownership and integration tests
Phase-out calculation complex for taxpayers in threshold ranges requiring detailed computations
State tax treatment varies; some states don't conform to federal QBI deduction creating state/federal differences
